name: Caterpillar Inc. v. Lewis
summary: Held that a final judgment need not be vacated for a jurisdictional defect at removal where the defect was cured before final judgment, balancing finality and efficiency against the plaintiff's choice of forum.
significance: Central precedent for both sides. Petitioner argued it supports preserving final judgments when only diverse parties remain; Respondent argued it only applies when the jurisdictional defect is cured before final judgment through voluntary dismissal.
